# UC-19.2.26 · VxRail LCM Compliance and Staged Bundle Drift

## Description

VxRail lifecycle compliance determines VMware and hardware driver supportability. Drift between staged bundles and installed release sets increases risk during one-click upgrades and delays security patching.

## Value

VxRail lifecycle compliance determines VMware and hardware driver supportability. Drift between staged bundles and installed release sets increases risk during one-click upgrades and delays security patching.

## Implementation

(1) Poll VxRail LCM inventory endpoints after each maintenance window; (2) alert on non-compliant or partially staged clusters; (3) export weekly compliance for VMware TAM reviews.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom (VxRail Manager REST API).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vxrail` `sourcetype="vxrail:lcm"` with fields `cluster_id`, `current_release`, `staged_release`, `compliance_state`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Poll VxRail LCM inventory endpoints after each maintenance window; (2) alert on non-compliant or partially staged clusters; (3) export weekly compliance for VMware TAM reviews.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:lcm" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(current_release) as cur, latest(staged_release) as staged, latest(compliance_state) as comp by cluster_id
| where comp!="Compliant" OR (isnotnull(staged_release) AND cur!=staged)
| table cluster_id, cur, staged, comp
| sort cluster_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**VxRail LCM Compliance and Staged Bundle Drift** — VxRail lifecycle compliance determines VMware and hardware driver supportability. Drift between staged bundles and installed release sets increases risk during one-click upgrades and delays security patching.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vxrail` `sourcetype="vxrail:lcm"` with fields `cluster_id`, `current_release`, `staged_release`, `compliance_state`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom (VxRail Manager REST API). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vxrail; **sourcetype**: vxrail:lcm. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vxrail, sourcetype="vxrail:lcm", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where comp!="Compliant" OR (isnotnull(staged_release) AND cur!=staged)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **VxRail LCM Compliance and Staged Bundle Drift**): table cluster_id, cur, staged, comp
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with VxRail Manager, VxVerify, and vCenter for the same cluster scope.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-compliant clusters), Bar chart (releases in fleet), Timeline (LCM state changes).

## SPL

```spl
index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:lcm" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(current_release) as cur, latest(staged_release) as staged, latest(compliance_state) as comp by cluster_id
| where comp!="Compliant" OR (isnotnull(staged_release) AND cur!=staged)
| table cluster_id, cur, staged, comp
| sort cluster_id
```

## Visualization

Table (non-compliant clusters), Bar chart (releases in fleet), Timeline (LCM state changes).
